_comment: Developer Workstation Setup - non-PowerShell layer (winget tools, VS Code extensions, environment). Consumed by Setup-Workstation.ps1 / the 'Workstation Setup' menu option. The PowerShell MODULE layer lives in Modules-Config.json.
_publicSafe: This file ships in the PUBLIC repo: NO secrets and NO organisation-specific values. Machine/org specifics (GHE host, git identity) come from an OPTIONAL, GIT-IGNORED 'workstation.local.json' that overrides the placeholders below.

tooling._note: winget package IDs (verified against the winget community repo). Installed with: winget install -e --id <id> --accept-package-agreements --accept-source-agreements
_postWinget: Claude Code is NOT a winget package. After Node installs, run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code   (or the native installer: irm https://claude.ai/install.ps1 | iex)

vscodeExtensions._note: Installed with: code --install-extension <id> --force. Themes / personal-preference extensions intentionally omitted.

git.user.name: <YOUR NAME>
git._lfs: Setup-Workstation.ps1 runs 'git lfs install' automatically once GitHub.GitLFS is present.

githubCli._hostNote: Override with your enterprise host in workstation.local.json (e.g. 'your-org.ghe.com'). Setup runs: gh auth login --hostname <host> --git-protocol https
githubCli._credentialHelper: After login, gh wires itself as the git credential helper for that host automatically.

envVars._note: Optional convenience env vars (user scope). Empty by default in the public repo.

consoleFont._note: OPT-IN conhost Oh My Posh / Nerd Font fix (Setup-ConsoleFont.ps1). Off by default - it touches the registry, needs admin for the HKLM allow-list step, and writes a managed UTF-8/OMP block into $PROFILE. Windows Terminal does not need this. Enable with the -ConsoleFont switch on Setup-Workstation.ps1, or set enabled:true here.
_winget: Microsoft.CascadiaCode (ships the 'Cascadia Code NF' Nerd Font)

moduleInstaller._note: After the workstation layer is set up, Setup-Workstation.ps1 invokes the PowerShell module installer in this same repo.

secretsChecklist._note: Printed at the end of setup. These are provisioned MANUALLY from your secure store - NEVER committed to any repo.
items: gh auth login              -> stores its token under the gh config (not in this repo)
claude login / OAuth       -> stores credentials under ~/.claude (not in this repo)
App-only auth certificate  -> import the .pfx into Cert:\CurrentUser\My from Key Vault / your secure store
az login                   -> interactive, or your service-principal flow
MCP server tokens / API keys -> configure locally per tool
